nodes.name: 西欧
nodes.summary: 西欧是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括红腹滨鹬、北方鲣鸟、红鸢，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 芬兰
nodes.summary: 芬兰是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括柳雷鸟、松鸡、翻石鹬等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 利比亚
nodes.summary: 利比亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括大红鹳、翎頜鴇、小凤头燕鸥等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 以色列
nodes.summary: 以色列是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括Sand partridge、石鸡、大红鹳等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 西亚
nodes.summary: 西亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联11种鸟类，包括小鸨、巨鹭、欧洲小雨燕等11种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 冈比亚
nodes.summary: 冈比亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联64种鸟类，包括Stone partridge、White-throated francolin、Ahanta spurfowl等64种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 歐洲俄羅斯
nodes.summary: 歐洲俄羅斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括红鸢，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 红鸢
nodes.summary: 红鸢（Red kite，学名 Milvus milvus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联8个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括农药污染、猎捕压力、森林砍伐。

nodes.name: 歐洲大陸
nodes.summary: 歐洲大陸是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括鵰鴞、红鸢，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The species currently breeds only in Europe, though it formerly also bred in West Asia and Northwest Africa. Historically, it was only resident in the milder parts of its range in Western Europe and Northwestern Africa, whereas all or most red kites in Northern mainland Europe wintered to the south and west, some also reaching western Asia, but an increasing number of northern birds now remain in that region year-round. Vagrants have reached north to Finland and south to Israel, Libya and the Gambia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Red kite in flight in Gredos Mountains, Avila, Spain Red kites inhabit broadleaf woodlands, pastures, mixed farmland, valleys and wetland edges, up to at least elevation. They are native to the western Palearctic, with all of the currently known 32,200–37,700 breeding pairs being in Europe. There also used to be breeding populations in western Asia (northern Iran, Syria and Turkey) and northwestern Africa (Algeria, Morocco and Tunisia), but most of these were extirpated in the 19th century or earlier; the only non-European breeding population in recent decades was in Morocco where the last known pair was in 2004. Today it breeds from Portugal and Spain, through the central part of the continent east to European Russia, north to southern Scandinavia, Latvia and the United Kingdom, and south to southern Italy; few if any breeders remain in the Balkans.{{cite journal | last1=Evans | first1=I. M

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Populations in Germany and France have subsequently stabilised, and because of growth in other countries, the overall population is now increasing. The main threats to red kites are poisoning, through illegal direct poisoning and indirect poisoning from pesticides, particularly in the wintering ranges in France and Spain, and changes in agricultural practices causing a reduction in food resources

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Other threats include electrocution, hunting and trapping, deforestation, egg-collection (on a local scale) and possibly competition with the generally more successful black kite M
